mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so a sustained easing path predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, so increases in unemployment over a short window predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST identifies strengthening final demand and housing activity, which should support cyclical earnings and lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-state signal does not outperform either series alone in out-of-sample tests, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in triggered high-demand episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in INDPRO indicates stronger real output and factory activity, which improves earnings expectations and supports near-term equity gains over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly sign accuracy on 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 52% over any 3-year rolling window, or if positive INDPRO momentum does not coincide with above-average returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero bound, real rates fall and liquidity conditions stay easy, which is bullish for S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3m annualized inflation > FEDFUNDS by 2pp and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-rate condition is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction term is insignificant in rolling 2-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in PAYEMS indicates payroll expansion and improving income flows, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in RSAFS signals weakening retail demand and softer earnings revisions, which should pressure the S&P 500 over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls month-over-month for 2 consecutive prints
failure_condition: Reject if triggered demand rollovers are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the mean return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE identifies a genuine labor-market deterioration, which should forecast negative S&P 500 returns over the next month more reliably than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal does not beat each marginal signal in out-of-sample ranking, or if negative 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of joint-deterioration episodes.
